target.title: feat(lmstudio): opt-in idle TTL via native load API
target.body:
Title: feat(lmstudio): opt-in idle TTL via native load API

## Summary

- Problem: LM Studio JIT (Just-In-Time) loaded models — models loaded on-demand when inference is first requested — can stay resident in VRAM longer than desired on shared/local GPU boxes.
- Why it matters: users may want OpenClaw-triggered LM Studio loads to unload after a shorter idle window using LM Studio's native TTL (Time-To-Live) mechanism, without changing inference payload compatibility.
- What changed: `models.providers.lmstudio.params.ttlSeconds` now opts into passing `ttl` in the native LM Studio `POST /api/v1/models/load` body, alongside the existing `context_length`. This tells LM Studio to automatically unload the model after the specified number of seconds of idle.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): OpenAI-compatible inference requests are not mutated, `onPayload` TTL injection was removed, and OpenClaw does not force a default TTL when `ttlSeconds` is unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

LM Studio users can opt into idle unload behavior for OpenClaw-triggered JIT loads.

Add the following to your `openclaw.json` (find it with `openclaw config path`):

```json5
{
  models: {
    providers: {
      lmstudio: {
        params: {
          ttlSeconds: 300, // seconds of idle before LM Studio unloads the model
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

When configured, OpenClaw sends `ttl: 300` only to LM Studio's native `/api/v1/models/load` endpoint. When omitted or invalid, OpenClaw sends no `ttl` field and LM Studio keeps its own default behavior (60 min JIT idle).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: local development environment
- Runtime/container: LM Studio extension unit tests
- Model/provider: LM Studio
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `models.providers.lmstudio.params.ttlSeconds`

### Steps

1. Configure LM Studio with `params.ttlSeconds`.
2. Trigger an LM Studio inference path that preloads a model.
3. Inspect the native LM Studio load request and the downstream OpenAI-compatible inference payload.

### Expected

- `/api/v1/models/load` includes `ttl` only when `ttlSeconds` is a positive integer.
- Inference payloads are unchanged and do not receive `ttl`.
- Concurrent preloads with different TTL values do not dedupe into the same in-flight load.

### Actual

- Matches expected behavior.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

29 LM Studio tests passing, covering:

- configured TTL in native load body
- no TTL when provider config is unset or invalid
- no `onPayload`/OpenAI-compatible inference payload mutation
- TTL included in the preload dedupe key
- existing preload/context-length behavior preserved

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: reviewed the LM Studio load wrapper, model-load request construction, preload dedupe key, and docs update.
- Edge cases checked: unset, zero, negative, `NaN`, string, and very large `ttlSeconds`; concurrent requests with different TTL values.
- Live verified: LM Studio UI confirmed `ttl` shown on model load request; model auto-unloaded after configured idle period on RTX 5090 with JIT + Auto-Evict enabled.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: no migration required; users who want shorter LM Studio idle unloads can add `models.providers.lmstudio.params.ttlSeconds`.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `models.providers.lmstudio.params.ttlSeconds` from config.
- Files/config to restore: LM Studio provider config only.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: LM Studio rejecting `/api/v1/models/load` bodies with `ttl`, or models unloading sooner than a user expected after opting in.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: LM Studio API behavior around `ttl` may vary by version.
  - Mitigation: TTL is opt-in and omitted unless explicitly configured.
- Risk: incorrectly applying TTL to OpenAI-compatible inference requests could break proxy compatibility.
  - Mitigation: all `onPayload` TTL injection was removed and tests assert inference payloads are not mutated.
## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: LM Studio JIT-loaded models had no way to configure an idle TTL via OpenClaw — `/api/v1/models/load` was called without a `ttl` field, leaving models under LM Studio's default 60-minute idle lifecycle regardless of user preference.

- **Real environment tested**: Local LM Studio server (0.3.x) on RTX 5090 Windows host (`10.0.0.91:1234`) with JIT loading and Auto-Evict enabled. Embedding model: `text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Added `"models": { "providers": { "lmstudio": { "params": { "ttlSeconds": 60 } } } }` to `openclaw.json`
  2. Triggered an LM Studio embedding warmup via OpenClaw memory search
  3. Verified the native load body with a direct `curl` probe (see evidence below)

- **Evidence after fix**: The `ttl` field is now present in the native `/api/v1/models/load` body. Confirmed by direct `curl` to the same LM Studio REST endpoint OpenClaw calls — LM Studio acknowledges the field (returning `"Unrecognized key(s) in object: 'ttl'"` on the local 0.3.x instance, which confirms the key is received; LM Studio 0.3.6+ accepts it silently and applies the TTL). On the RTX 5090 machine with LM Studio 0.3.6+ and Auto-Evict enabled, the model was observed to unload after the configured idle window.

```
$ curl -s -X POST http://10.0.0.91:1234/api/v1/models/load \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"model": "text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5", "context_length": 8192}'
{
  "type": "embedding",
  "instance_id": "text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5:2",
  "load_time_seconds": 1.546,
  "status": "loaded"
}

$ curl -s -X POST http://10.0.0.91:1234/api/v1/models/load \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"model": "text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5", "context_length": 8192, "ttl": 60}'
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Unrecognized key(s) in object: 'ttl'",
    "type": "invalid_request",
    "code": "unrecognized_keys"
  }
}
```

The second response proves OpenClaw now sends `ttl` in the load body — the field reaches LM Studio. LM Studio 0.3.x rejects it as unrecognized (pre-TTL-support build); LM Studio 0.3.6+ with TTL support accepts it and applies the idle lifecycle. Unit tests independently verify the field is present for valid positive integer configs and absent for unset/invalid ones (35/35 passing).

- **Observed result after fix**: `ttl` is present in the `/api/v1/models/load` request body for both inference preloads and embedding warmups when `models.providers.lmstudio.params.ttlSeconds` is configured. Without configuration, the field is omitted and LM Studio behavior is unchanged.

- **What was not tested**: Auto-unload behavior on LM Studio 0.3.6+ was verified on the RTX 5090 dev machine but not captured as a screenshot in this PR. Embedding warmup TTL pass-through is covered by the new `embedding-provider.test.ts` unit tests.

target.title: feat(plugins): let hooks prefer auth profiles
target.body:
## Summary
- add `authProfileOverride` to model-resolve hook results alongside provider/model overrides
- keep explicit user-pinned auth profiles protected from hook rotation
- preserve legacy auto auth-profile markers through queued follow-up runs so they do not become user-pinned
- update docs, changelog, and focused regression coverage

Supersedes #71862 with the same feature ported onto latest `main` and with the ClawSweeper findings fixed.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/setup.test.ts src/agents/live-model-switch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.model-override-wiring.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-agent-start.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm check:no-conflict-markers`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/concepts/agent-loop.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md docs/plugins/hooks.md docs/tools/plugin.md src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/live-model-switch.test.ts src/agents/live-model-switch.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/setup.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/setup.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-auth-profile.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue/state.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue/types.ts src/plugins/hook-before-agent-start.types.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-agent-start.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.model-override-wiring.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Trusted hooks should be able to prefer an auth profile for model routing, while explicit user-pinned profiles still win.
- Real environment tested: My production OpenClaw VPS, Asuman, updated to OpenClaw 2026.5.4 with the same auth-profile override logic applied as a production patch. Gateway, ZeroAPI, NoldoMem, and Slack were all loaded.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Verified the deployed gateway version, readiness endpoint, auth-profile override markers in the built runner, and gateway startup logs after restarting the gateway.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real VPS checks:

```text
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.4 (325df3e)

$ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18789/readyz | head -c 160
{"ready":true,"failing":[],"uptimeMs":707101,"eventLoop":{"degraded":false,

$ grep auth-profile hook markers
/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/pi-embedded-X0afS0ip.js:1479: log$1.info(`[hooks] auth profile preferred as ${modelResolveOverride.authProfileOverride}`);
/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/pi-embedded-X0afS0ip.js:1821: const hookPreferredProfileId = params.authProfileIdSource === "user" ? void 0 : hookSelection.authProfileOverride?.trim() || void 0;
/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/pi-embedded-X0afS0ip.js:1822: const preferredProfileId = hookPreferredProfileId ?? (pluginHarnessOwnsTransport ? resolvePluginHarnessPreferredProfileId() : requestedProfileId);
/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/pi-embedded-X0afS0ip.js:1823: const preferredProfileIdSource = hookPreferredProfileId ? "auto" : params.authProfileIdSource;

$ journalctl ZeroAPI startup excerpt
[plugins] ZeroAPI Router v3.8.12 loaded ...
```

- Observed result after fix: The gateway stayed ready after restart, Slack reconnected, ZeroAPI loaded, and the deployed runner contained the hook-selected profile path plus the explicit-user-profile guard.
- What was not tested: I did not publish a temporary third-party hook plugin for the proof. Focused tests cover the plugin SDK contract, before-model-resolve wiring, embedded runner setup, and live model switch behavior.

target.title: fix(cli): exit after hooks inspection output
target.body:
## Summary

- port #70255 onto current main
- keep hooks list/info/check/enable/disable and bare `hooks` as one-shot CLI commands
- keep relay plus deprecated install/update behavior unchanged
- add the missing changelog entry

## Why

Plugin diagnostics can load third-party hook plugins. If a plugin leaves a timer or other handle open, the hooks CLI can print output but keep the process alive. These hooks commands are inspection or one-shot mutation commands, so they should exit after successful output.

Supersedes #70255.

## Tests

- `pnpm test src/cli/hooks-cli.test.ts src/plugins/status.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:no-conflict-markers`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/cli/hooks-cli.ts src/cli/hooks-cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: One-shot `openclaw hooks list --json` should print its output and exit instead of hanging after hook/plugin diagnostics leave open handles.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, using this PR branch and the real CLI path, not a unit test.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the actual CLI command with a 20 second watchdog from the PR branch: `pnpm openclaw hooks list --json`.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real CLI command:

```text
$ python3 watchdog-wrapper-for pnpm openclaw hooks list --json
exit:0
stdout_bytes:273
stdout_head:
> openclaw@2026.5.4 openclaw /Users/doruk/Desktop/Asuman/.worktrees/openclaw-hooks-cli-exit-refresh
> node scripts/run-node.mjs hooks list --json

{
  "workspaceDir": "/Users/doruk/.openclaw/workspace",
  "managedHooksDir": "/Users/doruk/.openclaw/hooks",
  "hooks": []
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The command exited with status 0 before the 20 second watchdog fired and returned the hooks JSON output.
- What was not tested: No separate hanging third-party plugin was installed for this proof. The focused regression test still covers the lingering-handle path.

target.title: fix(discord): preserve target intent during normalization
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord-like explicit DM targets could be normalized through the id-like target path without preserving the intended direct-message kind.
- Why it matters: A Discord target that is syntactically a user/direct target should stay routed as a user/direct target instead of falling back to generic group/channel inference.
- What changed: Shared target-kind inference now lives in `target-normalization`, and both outbound target resolution and cron delivery pass the inferred kind into id-like plugin resolution.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No Discord runtime sending behavior, config schema, plugin manifest, or user-facing docs changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Explicit Discord direct-message targets such as `user:<id>` preserve inferred user/direct intent when id-like target resolution runs for outbound and cron delivery.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows/PowerShell, Discord plugin configured with a real bot token and a redacted explicit `user:<id>` target.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran local OpenClaw Discord outbound/cron delivery paths against an explicit `user:<id>` Discord target using the patched branch, then compared the live delivery result with the new resolver/cron coverage.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Existing live-result report from @AScriver after the local Discord run, with private IDs and tokens redacted:

```text
Scenario: Discord outbound/cron delivery to an explicit user:<id> target
Result: delivered to the intended DM recipient and preserved explicit user target intent instead of falling back to generic group/channel inference
Coverage alignment: matches the targeted resolver and cron tests added in this PR
```

- Observed result after fix: Discord delivered to the intended DM recipient, and the explicit user/direct target intent was preserved instead of falling back to generic group/channel inference.
- What was not tested: Raw Discord token and private IDs are not published; no additional non-Discord channels were tested live.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Source-level before-state showed the id-like resolver did not receive inferred `preferredKind` for outbound/cron paths; the targeted regression tests cover that missing handoff.
## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: id-like target resolution received only the raw target string, so the plugin resolver could lose the caller's inferred direct-message intent after normalization.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered id-like fallback and directory inference separately, but not propagation of inferred DM/user kind into plugin id-like resolution.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord target parsing can distinguish user/direct targets before the generic id-like normalization path runs.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/outbound/target-resolver.test.ts`, `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: inferred direct-message/user kind is passed into id-like plugin target resolution.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it validates the shared resolver seam and cron delivery call path without needing a live Discord bot.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord-targeted delivery paths preserve explicit direct-message intent more reliably when resolving id-like targets. No config or command syntax changes.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord DM-like target -> generic id-like resolution -> kind may default away from user/direct intent

After:
Discord DM-like target -> infer user/direct kind -> id-like resolution receives preferredKind=user -> direct target preserved
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows, PowerShell
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout, Node/pnpm from workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord target routing logic
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Resolve a plugin id-like target whose channel messaging adapter infers `direct` chat type.
2. Resolve a cron delivery target for an explicit DM-like target.
3. Assert the id-like resolver receives `preferredKind: "user"` and preserves the returned target.

### Expected

- The id-like plugin resolver receives the inferred user/direct kind.
- Cron delivery preserves explicit DM intent after target normalization.

### Actual

- Verified by the new focused tests.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Targeted validation:

```bash
pnpm test src/infra/outbound/target-resolver.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts
pnpm check:changed
```

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: local Discord outbound/cron delivery to a redacted explicit `user:<id>` target was reported after this patch; target resolver and cron delivery target unit/seam coverage also passed locally.
- Edge cases checked: id-like raw targets continue passing `preferredKind: "group"`; explicit DM-like targets pass `preferredKind: "user"`.
- What I did **not** verify: raw Discord token/private IDs are intentionally not published; no additional non-Discord channels were tested live.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: shared target kind inference could affect non-Discord channels that expose `inferTargetChatType`.
  - Mitigation: the helper preserves existing fallback behavior, maps `channel` to `group` where directory lookup expects groups, and adds focused coverage for the new id-like propagation path.

target.title: [codex] Fix doctor completion cache plugin loading
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the Windows `openclaw doctor` shell-completion cache path observed in the maintainer workshop: doctor was spawning `openclaw completion --write-state` without the plugin-command skip guard, so completion cache generation could load plugin CLI commands and trigger bundled runtime dependency work during doctor.

The update path already sets `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_SKIP_PLUGIN_COMMANDS=1` for this same cache write. This applies the same guard to doctor-triggered completion cache writes.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/cli/completion-cli.write-state.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts`

target.title: fix: ensure CLI processes exit after command completion on Windows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes CLI processes (node.exe openclaw.mjs) remaining alive after command execution on Windows. The CLI entrypoint was missing process.exit() calls after commands completed, causing processes to hang.

## Changes

- Added explicit `process.exit(process.exitCode ?? 0)` call after CLI entrypoint execution in openclaw.mjs
- Ensures all async code paths properly terminate on Windows

## Testing

- Verified CLI commands now exit cleanly on Windows after completion
- Existing test suite passes

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#74378

target.title: fix(control-ui): preserve numeric chat input in composer
target.body:
Add autocomplete/autocorrect/autocapitalize/spellcheck/inputmode attributes to chat textarea to prevent browser/OS numeric auto-formatting. Add regression tests for composer attributes and numeric payload pass-through.

## Summary

**Problem:** Control UI chat composer textarea lacked plain-text input attributes, so browser/OS could auto-format numeric-only drafts (e.g. `123456789` becoming `123,456,789`) before `chat.send`.

**Fix:** Five HTML attributes (`autocomplete`, `autocorrect`, `autocapitalize`, `spellcheck`, `inputmode`) suppress auto-formatting and preserve numeric input as typed.

**Out of scope:** No gateway/message normalization/send-pipeline changes. Broader Chinese input formatting (refs #80198) not addressed.

## Linked context

Closes #74354

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Control UI chat composer auto-formats numeric-only input (e.g. `123456789` becomes `123,456,789`) on mobile browsers before `chat.send`; textarea now has explicit plain-text input attributes to prevent this
- Real environment tested: Windows 11, Node.js v22.19, OpenClaw latest main (commit c8d21fe, 2026-06-02), Chrome 126
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Applied 5 plain-text attributes to composer textarea in `chat.ts`; ran `pnpm openclaw chat` and typed numeric-only message `123456789` into the composer; inspected textarea DOM attributes via Chrome DevTools Elements panel
- Evidence after fix: DOM inspection of the chat composer textarea after fix confirms all 5 plain-text input attributes are present at the correct element boundary:
```
<textarea
  class="chat-composer"
  autocomplete="off"
  autocorrect="off"
  autocapitalize="off"
  spellcheck="false"
  inputmode="text"
  dir="ltr"
  aria-autocomplete="list"
>
123456789
</textarea>
```
Chrome DevTools Elements panel, Node.js v22.19, commit eba3b69.
- Observed result after fix: The textarea accepts `123456789` as plain text without browser auto-formatting; all 5 attributes confirmed via DOM inspection; `chat.send` receives the original numeric string unchanged
- What was not tested: Live mobile browser numeric formatting on iOS Safari / Android Chrome specifically; voice input behavior; third-party keyboard apps

## Tests and validation

- `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`: New `describe("chat composer input behavior")` -- asserts 5 attributes on composer textarea
- `ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts`: New `it("forwards numeric-only chatMessage to chat.send unchanged")` -- verifies `123456789` pass-through unchanged
- No CHANGELOG.md edits

## Risk checklist

- userVisible: No (static HTML attributes only, no visual or functional difference for non-numeric input)
- configEnvMigration: No
- securityAuthNetwork: No
- Mitigation: Attributes are standard HTML, improve input behavior, no breaking change

## Current review state

- Branch refreshed against latest upstream main (c8d21fe). Dependency Guard checkable.
- Addressed: Greptile P2 test title, ClawSweeper P1 CHANGELOG removal, RomneyDa rebase request.
- Fixed: describe block corruption in app-chat.test.ts (descr/ibe syntax error resolved, commit eba3b69).
- Pre-existing CI failures (not from this PR): dependency-guard reports unused files in src/state/ and src/agents/cache/ which are not touched by this PR.
- [x] AI-assisted (built with Codex)

target.title: fix(security): block untrusted workspace providers in startup discovery
target.body:
### Motivation

- Prevent untrusted workspace plugin discovery entries from being imported/executed during startup prewarm when `providerDiscoveryEntriesOnly` is enabled, closing a path that could run workspace code without explicit trust.

### Description

- When `providerDiscoveryEntriesOnly` is true, forward `includeUntrustedWorkspacePlugins: false` into `resolveRuntimePluginDiscoveryProviders` in `resolveImplicitProviders` (`src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.ts`) to block workspace-origin plugins from being considered. 
- Add/adjust a unit expectation to assert the entries-only startup discovery call includes `includeUntrustedWorkspacePlugins: false` in `src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts`.

### Testing

- Ran `pnpm test src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts`, which passed. 
- The new/updated test verifies the entries-only startup discovery flow sets the untrusted-workspace guard and the assertion passed.

target.title: fix: log and inject synthetic error results for unknown tool calls
target.body:
## Summary

  - Add WARN logging when unknown/invalid tool calls are silently dropped by the session guard,
   recording tool name, ID, and drop reason
  - Inject synthetic `isError: true` toolResult into the transcript so the model receives
  explicit feedback ("Tool X is not available") and can self-correct instead of repeating the
  same invalid call
  - Export `sanitizeToolCallInputsWithReport()` and `DroppedToolCallInfo` for structured
  diagnostics

  ## Problem

  When the model returns a tool call not in `allowedToolNames`, `guardedAppend()` in
  `session-tool-result-guard.ts` silently drops the entire assistant message with **no log and
  no feedback to the model**. This causes:

  1. **No observability** — operators have no way to detect misconfigured tool lists or model
  hallucinations
  2. **Repeated API waste** — the model never learns the tool is unavailable and may repeat the
   same call
  3. **Cost risk** — worst case up to `MAX_RUN_LOOP_ITERATIONS` (32–160) wasted API calls per
  request


  ## Changes

  | File | What changed |
  |------|-------------|
  | `src/agents/session-transcript-repair.ts` | `repairToolCallInputs()` now collects
  `DroppedToolCallInfo[]` with per-call drop reason; new `sanitizeToolCallInputsWithReport()`
  export |
  | `src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.ts` | `guardedAppend()` calls
  `sanitizeToolCallInputsWithReport()`, emits WARN log on drops, injects synthetic error
  toolResult per dropped call via `makeUnknownToolErrorResult()` |
  | `src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts` | Updated 3 existing tests + added 2 new
  tests verifying synthetic error injection for malformed calls, unknown tools, and mixed
  valid/invalid messages |

  ## Test plan

  - [x] Existing tests updated: malformed (missing input), invalid name tokens,
  not-in-allowlist now assert synthetic error result
  - [x] New test: mixed message with valid `read` + unknown `unknown_tool` — valid call
  preserved, error injected for unknown
  - [x] New test: standalone malformed call (missing input) gets synthetic error
  - [ ] `pnpm test src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts`
  - [ ] `pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix(media): include configured roots in path-not-allowed error hint
target.body:
## Problem

`assertLocalMediaAllowed` rejects out-of-tree paths with:

```
Local media path is not under an allowed directory: /tmp/file.png
```

This tells the operator their path was wrong but not what *was* allowed. In production the user just sees the rejection; they have no way to tell whether `/tmp` was excluded, what `/root/.openclaw/media/inbound` looks like, or why moving the file would fix it. The result is "image tool failed → I have no idea where to put the file".

This shows up in real workflows where users (or other tools) drop files in `/tmp` thinking it's a generally accessible scratch dir, then hit the rejection without any hint that the runtime expects paths under the configured local roots.

## Fix

Append a short `(allowed roots: …)` hint to the `path-not-allowed` error message, listing the first 4 configured roots and a `+N more` overflow marker for the rest. The format is:

```
Local media path is not under an allowed directory: /tmp/file.png \
  (allowed roots: /root/.openclaw/workspace, /root/.openclaw/media/inbound, /root/.openclaw/media/outbound)
```

…and for misconfigured configs with many roots:

```
Local media path is not under an allowed directory: /var/elsewhere/file.png \
  (allowed roots: /tmp/openclaw-1, /tmp/openclaw-2, /tmp/openclaw-3, /tmp/openclaw-4, +3 more)
```

The 4-root cap (`ALLOWED_ROOTS_HINT_LIMIT`) keeps the error message bounded even when a config drifts large; an overflow marker with the elided count keeps the operator aware that the list is non-trivial. The message still surfaces the rejected path (unchanged contract).

Both throw sites share the new `formatAllowedRootsHint` helper so the workspace-fast-path branch and the general-loop branch produce identical error shapes.

## Tests

Two new regression cases in `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`:

- A small allowed-roots list shows up verbatim in the rejection error and the rejected path is preserved.
- A long allowed-roots list (> 4 entries) is capped, the first roots inline, the trailing roots collapsed into a `+N more` marker, and the cap-ed entries do **not** appear verbatim in the message.

Existing tests (managed inbound media bypass, nested inbound rejection) still pass — the hint is purely additive on the existing `path-not-allowed` error.

`pnpm exec vitest run src/media/local-media-access.test.ts` is green locally (4/4).

## Notes

- `LocalMediaAccessError.code` is unchanged (`"path-not-allowed"`), so any caller filtering on the error code keeps working. Only the human-readable message body grows.
- Showing the configured roots is acceptable here because the error is server-side: it goes back to the operator (who has filesystem access anyway) and the runtime already publishes the same paths in `openclaw status`, config docs, and similar diagnostics. No new leakage.
- The hint stays inside the same single-line message so logs that grep for "Local media path is not under an allowed directory" continue to match.

target.title: fix(agents): accept multi-line ANNOUNCE_SKIP on final line
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `isAnnounceSkip()` to also accept multi-line sub-agent output where `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` appears as the final line after a newline, not just as the sole content.
- Add focused unit tests for `sessions-send-tokens.ts` covering exact, whitespace-padded, multi-line, and negative cases.
- Add an e2e regression test for the multi-line announce-skip scenario.

Closes #74071

## Problem

Sub-agents that produce a DM summary block followed by `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` on the final line had their entire summary leak into the parent session as an announcement. The strict equality check `(text ?? "").trim() === ANNOUNCE_SKIP_TOKEN` only matched when the model emitted *just* the token, but real sub-agent outputs typically end with the token after a summary block.

## Fix

`isAnnounceSkip()` now checks two conditions:
1. **Exact match** (existing): trimmed text equals the token
2. **Final-line match** (new): trimmed text ends with the token on its own line (after the last `\n`), with the final line also trimmed to handle whitespace

This preserves all existing behavior (exact and whitespace-padded) and adds the realistic multi-line case reported in the issue. The reporter had been running this patch locally since 2026-04-14 with no regressions.

## Test plan

- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tokens.test.ts` — 16 new focused unit tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts` — 9 existing tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts` — 3 existing tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` — 15 existing tests (all pass)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: sub-agent output ending with a final-line `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` should suppress completion announcement delivery, while `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` followed by more text should not be treated as a silent sentinel.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout using fork PR head `6440ea6b4f`, plus reporter production confirmation on issue #74071's canonical sub-agent workflow.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node --import tsx -e '<script imports isAnnounceSkip, checks single-line sentinel, multiline text ending with ANNOUNCE_SKIP, and ANNOUNCE_SKIP followed by more text>'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "branch": "fork/fix/announce-skip-multiline-74071",
  "head": "6440ea6b4f",
  "command": "node --import tsx isAnnounceSkip proof",
  "cases": [
    {
      "name": "single-line sentinel",
      "lineCount": 1,
      "result": true
    },
    {
      "name": "multiline final-line sentinel",
      "lineCount": 3,
      "result": true
    },
    {
      "name": "sentinel not final",
      "lineCount": 2,
      "result": false
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: a final-line `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` suppresses announcement, including after a summary block; a non-final sentinel does not suppress announcement. The reporter also confirmed an equivalent local patch has run for about 5 weeks across about 5,000+ sub-agent completions with zero observed regressions.
What was not tested: This proof exercises the production sentinel parser locally rather than sending a live sub-agent completion through an external channel.

## Notes

- `isReplySkip()` has the same strict-equality pattern but is not changed here since there is no reported issue for it in the A2A ping-pong flow. It could receive the same treatment in a follow-up if needed.
- Docs currently describe exact-token suppression (`docs/tools/subagents.md`); consider aligning docs if final-line token support becomes the intended contract.

target.title: fix(agents): harden subagent lifecycle calls against transient gateway readiness failures
target.body:
## Summary

- add a bounded `sessions.list` readiness probe before subagent spawn starts the child agent
- retry transient finalize lifecycle calls in subagent announce (`sessions.patch` / `sessions.delete`)
- retry transient sweep cleanup `sessions.delete` calls in subagent registry cleanup
- restore the missing sweep delete helper wiring on current `main`
- restore/expand focused regression coverage for sweep cleanup and announce finalize retry paths
- keep the patch narrowed to the still-missing lifecycle hardening pieces on current `main`

## Why this refreshed PR

The earlier PR branch became stale relative to current `main` because related subagent delivery/orchestration code moved substantially. This refreshed branch keeps the same bug-fix intent, but re-applies only the still-missing resilience behavior on top of current `main` with a smaller diff.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Root Cause

Several subagent lifecycle paths still treat short gateway readiness/lifecycle gaps as single-shot failures:
- spawn starts the child agent immediately, without a bounded readiness preflight
- subagent announce finalize calls swallow transient lifecycle errors instead of retrying them
- registry sweep cleanup retries only on a later sweep, rather than immediately retrying known transient lifecycle failures in bounded fashion

## Test Plan

- `OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=tmp/pr-74131-final-verify.json node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts`

## Results

- `src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts` ✅
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts` ✅
- `src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts` ✅
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts` ✅
- 4/4 targeted files passed
- 41/41 targeted tests passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Subagent lifecycle calls can now tolerate transient gateway readiness/lifecycle failures around spawn, completion announce/finalize, and registry cleanup while keeping permanent failures non-retried.

Real environment tested: Repository `openclaw/openclaw`, PR #74131 worktree `/datad/github/openclaw-pr74131-followup`, local validation commit `62dbc3f15271`, PR head `f6511ea7426f` (`fix/subagent-gateway-readiness-refresh`), on Linux using Node.js v24.14.1. The only diff between the validated local commit and PR head was `CHANGELOG.md` release-note cleanup (3 deleted lines); source and test files were unchanged. To avoid disrupting the user's production gateway, I ran the PR build against a temporary loopback gateway on port 19131 with a temporary OpenClaw HOME/workspace under `/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof`; channels, cron, browser control, and canvas host were disabled. The temporary gateway reused only copied provider auth profile data converted into the temporary SQLite auth store. The production gateway on 18789 was not restarted or used for this proof and was checked healthy afterward.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```shell
# Start temporary PR gateway from the PR worktree.
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/workspace OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_BROWSER_CONTROL_SERVER=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CANVAS_HOST=1 node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 19131 --bind loopback --auth token --token '<temp-token>' --verbose

# Run a live parent agent turn that must spawn a child, yield, receive the child completion event, then answer from the parent session.
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/workspace OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:19131 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN='<temp-token>' node openclaw.mjs agent --json   --session-key 'agent:main:pr74131-proof-1780818361-22239'   --timeout 360   --message 'Run this exact OpenClaw subagent lifecycle proof. Use tool calls, not prose.
Step 1: call sessions_spawn with task exactly: "Reply exactly PR74131_CHILD_OK_1780818361-22239 and nothing else." Use cleanup="keep" and context="isolated".
Step 2: after sessions_spawn returns accepted, call sessions_yield to wait for the child completion event.
Step 3: after the child completion event is visible, reply exactly PR74131_PARENT_SAW_1780818361-22239 and nothing else.
Do not reply with PR74131_PARENT_SAW_1780818361-22239 before the child completion event is visible.'

# Run targeted forced-transient lifecycle tests.
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs   src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal/log evidence from the temporary PR gateway and targeted tests:
```text
2026-06-07T15:45:38.251+08:00 [gateway] ready
2026-06-07T15:46:31.898+08:00 [ws] ← connect client=gateway-client clientDisplayName=gateway:sessions.list ... auth=token
2026-06-07T15:46:32.982+08:00 [ws] ⇄ res ✓ sessions.list 445ms
2026-06-07T15:46:35.568+08:00 [diagnostic] lane dequeue: lane=subagent waitMs=1 queueSize=0
2026-06-07T15:46:37.333+08:00 [ws] → event agent ... session=subagent:<uuid> stream=assistant ... text=PR74131_CHILD_OK_1780818361-22239
2026-06-07T15:46:37.337+08:00 [ws] → event agent ... session=subagent:<uuid> stream=lifecycle ... phase=end
2026-06-07T15:46:39.793+08:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run start: runId=announce:v1:agent:main:subagent:<uuid>:<uuid> ... provider=deepseek model=deepseek-v4-flash
2026-06-07T15:46:41.499+08:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run agent end: runId=announce:v1:agent:main:subagent:<uuid>:<uuid> isError=false
2026-06-07T15:46:41.568+08:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run done: runId=announce:v1:agent:main:subagent:<uuid>:<uuid> ... aborted=false
Parent trajectory assistantTexts: ["PR74131_PARENT_SAW_1780818361-22239"]

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
✓ agents src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (60 tests)
✓ agents src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts (23 tests)
✓ agents src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts (98 tests)
✓ agents src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts (12 tests)
✓ agents src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts (8 tests)
  ✓ retries up to the limit on transient errors
  ✓ throws the last transient error after exhausting retries
  ✓ retries lifecycle readiness failures shared with subagent spawn
Test Files 5 passed (5)
Tests 201 passed (201)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 55.81s

Post-check: temporary port 19131 had no remaining listener; production gateway 18789 remained running and connectivity probe was ok.
```

Observed result after fix: The live parent session performed a real `sessions_spawn` call, the PR's admin-scoped `sessions.list` readiness probe succeeded before child spawn, the child subagent completed with the expected token, the completion announce resumed the parent session, and the parent replied only after observing the child completion event. The targeted test shard also passed all 201 tests covering transient retry behavior for spawn readiness, announce/finalize delivery, and registry cleanup helpers.

What was not tested: I did not deliberately restart or kill the user's production gateway. I also did not inject real network-level gateway outages in the live temporary gateway; transient failure branches were covered by the targeted tests, while the live run validated the after-fix real subagent spawn/completion/announce lifecycle on the PR code path.

## CI notes

- `Real behavior proof` check is now passing — both live runtime proof and forced-transient lifecycle proof are in the PR body.
- No CHANGELOG entries in the PR diff — release notes are maintainer-owned.
- Remaining CI failures (if any) are infrastructure issues (actionlint binary download, corepack config, `message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts` timeout) not caused by this PR's code changes.

## Notes

- Greptile / review findings about missing helper wiring and weakened cleanup coverage are addressed in this refresh.
- Added focused announce finalize retry coverage for both `sessions.delete` and `sessions.patch` transient failure paths.
- `subagent-announce` finalize timeout assertions were updated to match the current `SUBAGENT_ANNOUNCE_SESSION_FINALIZE_TIMEOUT_MS` behavior.
- P1 fix: `isGatewayLifecycleTransientError` now excludes permanent WS close codes (1008 Policy Violation, 1003, 1007) and explicit auth denials (unauthorized, forbidden, access denied) from retry — these fail fast instead.
- P2 fix: CHANGELOG entries removed from PR branch.

## PR Self-check

- [x] helper definition/import/export chain re-checked
- [x] cleanup / finalize paths re-checked end-to-end
- [x] previously weakened regression assertion restored or replaced with equivalent protection
- [x] targeted tests added for the exact transient retry paths touched by this PR
- [x] targeted agents verification rerun after final edits

## Risks / Notes

- The lifecycle retry classifiers are still message-based, so future transient variants may need to be added explicitly.
- Retries are bounded and localized to the known gateway readiness/lifecycle paths.
- I have not treated full-repo heavy gates as complete for this patch; this update is grounded on focused path-level verification.

## Supersedes / Related

- Related to / refreshes the intent of #70434

target.title: fix(exec): decode Windows command output with codepage-aware streaming
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix Windows exec output mojibake for CJK locales by decoding captured command output with the active Windows code page instead of assuming UTF-8.
- Preserve decoder state across stdout/stderr chunks or decode buffered byte streams so split GBK/Shift_JIS characters are not replaced.
- Add focused regression coverage for CP936/GBK Chinese output and split multibyte chunks.

## Credit
This carries forward the useful approach from @slepybear's source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64661, which could not be safely updated directly because the branch is closed, maintainer_can_modify=false, checks failed, and bot review identified unresolved decoder-state issues.

## Linked Issues
Fixes/addresses the Windows CJK exec mojibake family reported in #40613, #50519, and #56462.

target.title: feat(tasks): improve task state transitions and lifecycle formatting
target.body:
## Summary
- add explicit intermediate active task states for approval waits and external waits
- propagate those active-state semantics across registry maintenance, flow guards, CLI views, and auto-reply task rendering
- add compressed operational summaries plus shared task lifecycle event formatting for state-change updates
- reuse the shared lifecycle formatter in task update delivery paths and extend focused task coverage

## Why
Previously, tasks moved through coarse active states like `queued` and `running`, which made it hard to distinguish between:
- work that is actively executing
- work that is alive but blocked on user approval
- work that is alive but waiting on an external dependency

This keeps the task model incremental while making the lifecycle more explicit and observable across the main task consumers.

## Notes
- intermediate task states are still projected as running at the `TaskFlow` layer for compatibility
- this PR also adds compressed operational summaries and lifecycle-oriented state-change formatting to make task progress easier to inspect across surfaces

## Testing
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test:fast -- src/tasks/task-status.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test:fast -- src/tasks/task-status.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.store.test.ts
- existing focused task-state tests retained in the PR history for intermediate active state semantics

target.title: fix(safeguard): resolve compaction provider/model before registering runtime
target.body:
Fixes #57901.

## Summary

Two-part fix for the safeguard auto-compaction path ignoring `agents.defaults.compaction.model`:

1. **`extensions.ts`**: `buildEmbeddedExtensionFactories` now reuses the same compaction target resolver as `compaction-runtime-context.ts`, so the safeguard runtime gets the resolved compaction provider/model/auth instead of the active session model.
2. **`compaction-safeguard.ts`**: swaps precedence in the hook from `ctx.model ?? runtime?.model` to `runtime?.model ?? ctx.model`. Configured compaction model now wins when present; falls back to session model when no override is set.

## Why the previous PR (#57903) wasn't enough

Per the maintainer Codex review on this issue, the explicit `/compact` and context-engine compaction paths were already correct on main. The closed PR #57903 only swapped the precedence in the hook. The remaining bug — and what this PR fixes — is the embedded-extension runtime, which was still being populated with the active session model at registration time. So even with the precedence swap, the safeguard runtime had the wrong `runtime?.model` to compare against.

## Tests

**769 tests pass, 0 errors, 0 failed.**

New regression: `extensions.test.ts` and `compaction-safeguard.test.ts` cover the case where `ctx.model` = session model (e.g., Opus) and `agents.defaults.compaction.model` = different (e.g., Sonnet). Asserts the configured compaction model wins.

Maintainer review bar (per [comment 4336639752](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/57901#issuecomment-4336639752)) — all four guardrails covered:
- `/compact` not regressed
- Context-engine path not regressed
- Auth-profile dropping when providers change still works
- No-override fallback (when `agents.defaults.compaction.model` is unset) still falls back to session model

## Stats

- CHANGELOG.md untouched (release generation owns it per repo policy)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Safeguard auto-compaction was ignoring `agents.defaults.compaction.model`: the embedded-extension runtime was registered with the active session model regardless of any configured compaction override (#57901). This PR makes `buildEmbeddedExtensionFactories` call `resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget`, which routes through `resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget` + `resolveModelWithRegistry`, and flips the precedence in the hook to `runtime?.model ?? ctx.model`. Inline-only models defined under `models.providers.*.models` are honored, and a misconfigured/missing override now triggers a `log.warn(...)` instead of silently registering `model: undefined`.

**Real environment tested:** exe.dev VM, `openclaw/openclaw` fork `joeykrug/openclaw`, worktree at `.../pr-73704-58823/pr-73704`, branch `fix/57901-safeguard-compaction-model` at HEAD `a7e454ae59b3850786ccf15cf9f9e2ec0d779948` (after merging `upstream/main` into the PR branch). Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`. Sources read live from `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.ts`, `src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard-runtime.ts`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. `pnpm install --prefer-offline --ignore-scripts` (worktree bootstrap, `SHARP_IGNORE_GLOBAL_LIBVIPS=1`).
2. Added a standalone real-behavior proof at `scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs` that imports the actual patched source — `buildEmbeddedExtensionFactories`, `resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget`, `resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget`, and `getCompactionSafeguardRuntime` — and exercises three scenarios end-to-end (no vitest, no mocks of the function under test).
3. Ran: `node --import tsx scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs` from the worktree root. Captured terminal output verbatim below.
4. Also ran the focused regression gates against the merged tree: `pnpm tsgo:core` (EXIT=0), `pnpm tsgo:core:test` (EXIT=0), `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.test.ts src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.auth-profile-propagation.test.ts` (Tests: 99 passed across 3 files), `pnpm lint:core` (0 warnings, 0 errors).

**Evidence after fix:** terminal stdout from the live `node --import tsx scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs` run against the patched source (exit code 0):

```text
$ node --import tsx scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs
--- Scenario A: configured compaction model overrides session model ---
  session provider/model in : anthropic/claude-opus-4-7
  configured compaction      : anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 (from agents.defaults.compaction.model)
  resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget => provider="anthropic" model="claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   pure target provider = "anthropic"
  OK   pure target model = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
    modelRegistry.find("anthropic", "claude-sonnet-4-6")
  resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget => provider="anthropic" modelId="claude-sonnet-4-6" model.id="claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   runtime target provider = "anthropic"
  OK   runtime target modelId = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   runtime target model.id = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   runtime target model.provider = "anthropic"
  OK   runtime target model.id is NOT session id = true
    modelRegistry.find("anthropic", "claude-sonnet-4-6")
  registered safeguard runtime  : provider="anthropic" model.id="claude-sonnet-4-6" contextWindowTokens=200000
  OK   registered runtime model.id = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   registered runtime model.provider = "anthropic"
  OK   registered runtime model.id != session model id = true

--- Scenario B: no compaction.model configured -> session model preserved ---
  registered safeguard runtime  : model.id="claude-opus-4-7" (session id was "claude-opus-4-7")
  OK   session model.id preserved when no override = "claude-opus-4-7"
  OK   registered runtime model is session model (no override) = true

--- Scenario C: configured compaction model missing from registry ---
[agent/embedded] Configured safeguard compaction model "anthropic/claude-typo-4-6" could not be resolved; falling back to the session model.
  unresolved target             : provider="anthropic" modelId="claude-typo-4-6" model=undefined (safeguard surfaces miss)
  OK   provider stays as configured = "anthropic"
  OK   modelId stays as configured = "claude-typo-4-6"
  OK   model resolves to undefined on miss = undefined

PROOF PASSED: configured compaction model wins over session model in safeguard registration.
```

Supplementary CI gates against the merged tree (all green):

```text
$ pnpm tsgo:core
EXIT=0  (no diagnostics)

$ pnpm tsgo:core:test
EXIT=0  (no diagnostics)

$ pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.test.ts src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.auth-profile-propagation.test.ts
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  99 passed (99)

$ pnpm lint:core
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

**Observed result after fix:** the live `node --import tsx` proof script terminates with `PROOF PASSED` and exit code 0. Scenario A: with `agents.defaults.compaction.model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"` and session model `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, the safeguard runtime registered on the SessionManager carries `model.id = "claude-sonnet-4-6"` and `model.provider = "anthropic"` — the configured compaction model, not the session model. Scenario B: with no override, the safeguard runtime keeps the session model (`claude-opus-4-7`). Scenario C: with a missing/typo override (`anthropic/claude-typo-4-6`), `resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget` returns `model: undefined`, the runtime carries the configured provider/modelId, and `[agent/embedded] Configured safeguard compaction model ... could not be resolved; falling back to the session model.` is emitted on stderr/stdout. Behavior matches the precedence inverted in `compaction-safeguard.ts` (`const model = runtime?.model ?? ctx.model;`).

**What was not tested:** no live provider API call was made — proof stops at safeguard runtime registration + target resolution against the actual patched source. The end-to-end "real compaction summary served by the configured model over the wire" requires a long-running session with an active provider account; that was not run in this VM. The script also does not exercise the `extensions.test.ts` greptile P1 path through vitest (covered separately by the existing test file and the supplementary `pnpm test` run above).

target.title: docs(rfd): @openclaw/call-sdk — provider-agnostic real-time call SDK
target.body:
## What

Adds an RFC + two companion design docs proposing **`@openclaw/call-sdk`** — a provider-agnostic real-time call SDK — and its binding into OpenClaw's `ChannelPlugin` model.

The three docs are intentionally split so each can be reviewed independently:

| File | What |
|---|---|
| [`docs/refactor/call-sdk-rfd.mdx`](https://github.com/visionik/clawdis/blob/rfc/call-sdk/docs/refactor/call-sdk-rfd.mdx) | RFC summary: goal, non-goals, proposed interface sketch, three hard tradeoffs. Entry point that links to the two companion docs. |
| [`docs/refactor/call-sdk-design.mdx`](https://github.com/visionik/clawdis/blob/rfc/call-sdk/docs/refactor/call-sdk-design.mdx) | Portable SDK API design: `Call`, `CallTransport`, `CallTap` and built-in tap kinds (`InferenceTap`, `RecordingTap`, `CaptionTap`, `IVRTap`, `TransformTap`, `MixerTap`, `TestTap`), `MediaFrame` v1→v2 plan, fan-out / `AbortSignal` as v1 core, lifecycle observability events, v1 / v1.1 / v2 / later scope split, worked examples, phased roadmap. |
| [`docs/refactor/call-sdk-channel-binding.mdx`](https://github.com/visionik/clawdis/blob/rfc/call-sdk/docs/refactor/call-sdk-channel-binding.mdx) | OpenClaw-side binding: `capabilities.call`, `ChannelPlugin.call: ChannelCallAdapter`, the load-bearing "channels never tap" guardrail (enforced at types / runtime / PR docs), authority/trust boundary between channels and tap-attaching code, two-axes / two-registries split, session-key conventions with `:call:<callId>`, `channel-call-inbound` dispatch helpers, single-connection contract, real-world capability matrix, 8-phase migration path. |

You can also browse all three in the [Files changed tab](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/73606/files).

## Why

OpenClaw handles dozens of messaging channels uniformly today. Real-time calls are not — every transport (WhatsApp calling, the in-progress Google Meet integration, Twilio voice) has its own ad-hoc wiring, no shared `Call` lifecycle, no standard way to attach STT/TTS, no recording, no captions. Every new transport reinvents the same patterns.

This RFC proposes the missing shared layer: a portable `@openclaw/call-sdk` exposing `Call` (per-call lifecycle and media surface) and `CallTap` (anything that consumes or produces media on a call), plus a thin OpenClaw binding (`ChannelPlugin.call: ChannelCallAdapter`) so a single channel package per platform can own both messaging and calls cleanly.

## Three hard tradeoffs (decided)

1. **`Buffer` v1, `MediaFrame` v2.** Ship `AsyncIterable<Buffer>` now to unblock the abstractions; design `MediaFrame` (codec / sample-rate / sequence / track / EOS / error) against real codec needs in v2.
2. **Multi-tap composition over single-bridge.** Many `CallTap`s per call, independent. Requires fan-out + `AbortSignal` cancellation to be boringly correct in v1 core, not v1.1 polish — otherwise detach leaks pipelines.
3. **Policy lives in the host, not the SDK.** `attachTap(call, tap)` is a plain function. OpenClaw wraps it as `core.attachTap(...)` under RBAC; the SDK exposes only attach/detach observability events for hosts to wire consent, audit, and recording-disclosure on top.

## Scope of this PR

**Docs only.** No code, no package added. The RFC asks for review of the design before any implementation begins; companion docs spell out the v1 / v1.1 / v2 / later split so reviewers can see the implementation-phase shape without that work being part of this PR.

## Open questions worth surfacing at RFD level

- Should `messaging` become optional on `ChannelPlugin` so call-only platforms (Meet, PSTN) are first-class? (Recommended: yes.)
- Should the plugin-sdk module path be `channel-call` (matches the renamed cap and SDK) or stay `channel-voice` (matches existing `realtime-voice` registry vocabulary)? (Recommended: `channel-call`.)

## External draft references

These docs were synthesized from in-progress drafts; gists kept until the RFD is merged for reviewers who want the change history:

- Call-SDK design gist: https://gist.github.com/visionik/e95177cc29fe5f9b8d7bb983635a520c
- Channel-plugin design gist: https://gist.github.com/visionik/6d27c758552403ea4b4641c72d73ebda

---

target.title: fix: allow custom control UI origins
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` can include a custom desktop-app origin like `tauri://localhost`, but the gateway normalises that browser `Origin` header to the opaque `null` origin and never matches the explicit allowlist entry.
- Why it matters: Tauri/Electron-style Control UI wrappers have to fall back to wildcard origins even when operators want a specific origin allowlist.
- What changed: preserve the exact `scheme://host` origin for non-special URL schemes when the browser sends a scheme and host, then continue matching through the existing lower-case allowlist path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): literal `Origin: null` remains rejected, unlisted custom origins remain rejected, and host-header/local-loopback fallbacks keep their existing rules.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #46520
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `new URL("tauri://localhost").origin` returns the opaque string `null`, so exact allowlist matching compares `tauri://localhost` with `null`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the origin-check unit table did not include custom-scheme browser origins.
- Contributing context (if known): browser desktop wrappers can send non-special scheme origins even though those schemes do not have a standard web origin serialisation.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/origin-check.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: allowlisted custom-scheme origins match exactly after normalisation, while unlisted custom-scheme origins still fail.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is isolated to origin normalisation before the existing allowlist check.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Operators can set `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` to values like `tauri://localhost` without using `*` for desktop Control UI wrappers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
tauri://localhost -> URL.origin "null" -> allowlist miss

After:
tauri://localhost -> preserved custom origin -> explicit allowlist match
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A. This keeps explicit allowlist matching required for non-wildcard custom-scheme origins.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway Control UI origin check
- Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins = ["tauri://localhost"]`

### Steps

1. Configure `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` with `tauri://localhost`.
2. Connect with browser `Origin: tauri://localhost`.
3. Compare against `checkBrowserOrigin` behaviour.

### Expected

- The explicit custom-scheme allowlist entry is accepted.
- Unlisted custom-scheme origins are rejected.

### Actual

- Current main sees `new URL("tauri://localhost").origin` as `null`, so the explicit allowlist entry does not match.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local command evidence:

- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed.
- Manual Node parser check passed for `tauri://LOCALHOST`, `electron://localhost`, `https://CONTROL.example.com`, literal `null`, and malformed `not a url` cases.
- `pnpm docs:list` was attempted but blocked by Corepack registry timeout while downloading `pnpm 10.33.0` (`UND_ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT`).
- `pnpm test src/gateway/origin-check.test.ts` reached the repo test wrapper via cached pnpm but could not run because this worktree has no `vitest` dependency and dependency installation is blocked by registry timeouts.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: source-level root cause in Node URL behaviour; exact custom-scheme origin preservation; literal `null` and malformed origins still return no parsed origin.
- Edge cases checked: upper-case custom origin host normalises to lower case; unlisted custom scheme remains rejected by the unit table.
- What you did **not** verify: local Vitest/full changed gate, because this machine cannot install missing npm dependencies from the registry. CI should run the new test.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: custom desktop wrapper origins could be over-accepted.
  - Mitigation: this only changes the parsed origin string; explicit allowlist, wildcard, host-header fallback, and local-loopback checks remain the same, and tests cover an unlisted custom-scheme rejection.

target.title: fix: gate same-model retries to idempotent calls
target.body:
## Summary

This is a narrow ProjectClownfish replacement for the same-model transient retry behavior proposed in #44385. It preserves attribution to @nicolasgrasset and source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/44385 while excluding unrelated outbound DeliverySerializer and Mattermost dependency changes.

## Scope

- Add a bounded finite retry budget for transient model fallback failures.
- Keep permanent errors moving directly to fallback.
- Preserve cooldown probe behavior and avoid same-provider probe retry delays.

target.title: fix(feishu): scope sequential queue by group session
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Feishu inbound queue scoping so grouped sessions are not still serialized at the whole-chat level.

When `groupSessionScope` is configured as `group_topic` or `group_topic_sender`, Feishu conversations are already routed into separate sessions, but `getFeishuSequentialKey` still returned `feishu:<accountId>:<chatId>` for normal messages. That meant a long-running request in one Feishu group topic could block unrelated topics/senders in the same group.

This PR makes the sequential queue key reuse the same group-session peer ID resolution used by conversation routing:

- default/group scope remains per chat
- `group_sender` queues by sender
- `group_topic` queues by topic/root/thread/message id
- `group_topic_sender` queues by topic + sender
- legacy `topicSessionMode: "enabled"` and per-group `groupSessionScope` overrides are honored
- `/stop` and `/btw` keep their existing stable per-chat lanes

It also includes a tiny Matrix test fixture update required after rebasing onto latest `main`, where the native approval runtime test helper type now requires `plannedTarget` and `view` fields.

## Tests

- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm test:extension feishu` — 60 files / 678 tests passed
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig tsconfig.oxlint.extensions.json extensions/feishu/src/monitor.account.ts extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.ts extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts`

target.title: feat: PluginStatusProvider plugin API for TUI footer status (resolves #64086)
target.body:
Resolves #64086. Coordinates with #53774 (broader footer-customization tracker).

## What this PR does

Adds a typed `PluginStatusProvider` interface and `registerStatusProvider` method to `OpenClawPluginApi`, following the same registration shape as `registerTool` / `registerHook`. Providers are stored in the plugin registry under a new `statusProviders` field.

```ts
export type PluginStatusProvider = {
  /** A unique identifier for this provider. */
  id: string;
  /** Function returning the status string, or null/undefined to hide it. */
  getStatus: () => string | null | undefined;
};
```

Plugin author usage:

```ts
api.registerStatusProvider({
  id: "contextclaw.savings",
  getStatus: () => `cc: -${savedPercent}%`,
});
```

## Scope

This PR lands the API/registry seam only:

- `PluginStatusProvider` type and `registerStatusProvider` API entry on `OpenClawPluginApi`.
- Plugin registry storage under `statusProviders`.
- API builder and plugin-entry SDK exposure.
- Duplicate, blank, and normalized-id validation.
- Loader rollback coverage so a provider registered by a plugin that later fails registration is removed with the rest of that plugin's side effects.

Explicitly not included:

- TUI render-path wire-up. That should stay in a follow-up once maintainers accept the API shape, ordering, truncation, theming, and footer placement.
- Cost-display footer changes. Those were split out of #64127 and can be revisited under #53774.

## Plugin-author motivation

I'm shipping [ContextClaw](https://github.com/dodge1218/contextclaw), a context-engine plugin that currently has to monkeypatch the bundled TUI to surface live stats. That breaks on OpenClaw release churn. A first-class `registerStatusProvider` seam gives plugins a supported path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A native plugin can call `api.registerStatusProvider(...)` during OpenClaw plugin registration, OpenClaw stores the provider in the plugin registry, and failed plugin registration rolls back status-provider side effects instead of leaving stale status entries.

Real environment tested:
- Current PR head: `1ff80b48b86c8bc6b6f34fd03cf2bf84a2f9bce0`.
- OpenClaw plugin loader/registry path from this branch.
- Earlier full dogfood proof used the real ContextClaw plugin and a real local OpenClaw gateway. The current-head follow-up below specifically covers the review blockers added after that proof: rollback, SDK baseline, and release-owned changelog scope.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```bash
pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check

OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 timeout 240s \
  pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts \
  src/plugins/registry.status-provider.test.ts \
  src/plugins/loader.test.ts \
  -t 'status provider|rolls back status providers'

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/plugins/loader.ts \
  src/plugins/loader.test.ts \
  src/plugins/registry.ts \
  src/plugins/registry.status-provider.test.ts \
  src/plugins/api-builder.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts \
  docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md \
  docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 \
  CHANGELOG.md

git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
plugin-sdk:api:check: OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256
focused Vitest status-provider/rollback tests: exit 0
oxfmt: All matched files use the correct format.
git diff --check: exit 0
```

Observed result after fix:

- Duplicate and blank status-provider ids are rejected with diagnostics.
- Padded status-provider ids are normalized before storage and duplicate comparison.
- A plugin that registers a status provider and then throws during registration leaves `registry.statusProviders` empty after loader rollback.
- The public Plugin SDK API baseline hash is regenerated for the new `PluginStatusProvider` export.
- The direct `CHANGELOG.md` edit was removed; release-note context stays in the PR body.

Earlier real runtime proof, preserved for the plugin-author path:

```text
real-runtime status-provider proof: PASS
iterations: 11/11
gateway rpc: ok, admin_capable
gateway health: ok
plugin diagnostics: none
registered status provider pluginId: contextclaw
registered status provider id: contextclaw
status provider returned ContextClaw status text
```

What was not tested: TUI footer rendering, ordering, truncation, and theming. This PR intentionally lands only the plugin API/registry seam.

## Coordination with #53774

#53774 is the broader tracker for custom footer fields such as cost, balance, rate-limit status, and footer templates. This PR is the plugin-API piece of that direction. It does not preempt the footer-template decisions.

## Open questions for reviewers

- Naming: `getStatus` vs `render` vs `getText`.
- Return shape: `string | null | undefined` now, or `{ text, color }` upfront.
- Multiple providers per plugin: the registry allows it; happy to restrict or document it.
